sutta: once when the exalted 1 was staying near sabahti at jetta grove in anata pindika spa the parable sarikuta came and visited him saluted and sat down at 1 side so seated the vulnerable sarikuta said to the exalted 1 lord i have spent the rainy season here in savathi and i now wish to go into the country and the buddha said now is the time sarah gupta to do as you think fit so the venable sariputta got up saluted and took his departure keeping the exalted 1 on his right now not long after his departure a certain monk addressed the exalted 1 and said lord the variable sarikuta has offended me and without asking my pardon has set out on a journey and the exhorted 1 said to among and in my name did sarikuta come saying the teacher calls you sarikuta [Music] yes lord replied the monk and he went and gave the variable sarikuta the exalted ones message yes reverend sir he replied then when the verbal maha mogalana and the vulnerable ananda put their keys from lodging to lodging saying he sent you references and come for the vulnerable sarikuta will now draw his lion's raw in the presence of the exalted 1 and the verbal sarikuta went to the exalted 1 and saluted him and sat down at 1 side and the exalted 1 said to him there is here sariputta a fellow wayfarer who has a grievance he says lord the variable has offended me and without asking my pardon has set out on a journey and the verbal salputa said true it is lord that he in whom mindfulness of the body's actions is not present might set out on a journey without asking pardon of a fellow wayfarer in the holy life whom he has offended lord just as on the earth they cast things clean and foul dung urine spiritual past and blood yet for all that the earth is not filled with horror lord even so lord like the earth i abide with mine large abundant measureless feeling no hatred nor ill will true it is lord that he in whom mindfulness is not present might set out on a journey without asking pardon of a fellow wayfarer whom he had offended lord just as in water they wash things clean and foul dung urine spiritual pus and blood yet for all that the water is not filled with horror loathing or disgust even so lord that water i abide with mine large abundant measureless feeling no hatred nor ill will true it is lord that he in whom mindfulness is not present might set out on a journey without asking pardon of a fellow wayfarer whom he had offended lord jesus fire burns things clean and foul dung urines little puss and blood yet for all that the fire is not filled with horror loathing or disgust even so lord like fire i abide with mine large abundant measureless feeling no hatred nor ill will true it is lord that he in whom mindfulness is not present might set out on a journey without asking pardon of a fellow wayfarer whom he had offended lord just as the wind blows on things clean and fall down urine's little busts and blood yet for all that the wind is not filled with horror loathing or disgust even so lord like the wind i abide with mine large abundant measureless feeling no hatred nor ill will true it is lord that he in whom mindfulness is not present might set out on a journey without asking pardon of a fellow wayfarer whom he had offended lord just as a duster wipes up things clean and foul dung urine's little pus and blood yet for all that the duster is not filled with horror loathing or disgust even so lord like a duster i abide with mine large abundant it's measureless feeling no hatred nor ill will true it is lord that he in whom mindfulness is not present might set out on a journey without asking pardon of a fellow wayfarer whom he had offended lord this as a boy or girl of the scavenger class clad in rags with begging trey in hand on entering village or town assumes a humble man and then goes in even so lord like a scavenger boy i abide with mine large abundant measureless feeling no hatred nor ill will true it is lord that he in whom mindfulness is not present might set out on a journey without asking pardon of a fellow wayfarer whom he had offended lord just as a bull would cut horns mild wild well-tamed well-trained roaming from street to street from crossroad to crossroad harms nothing with its feet or horns even so lord like a bull with cut horns i abide with mine large abundant measureless feeling no hatred nor ill will true it is lord that he in whom mindfulness is not present might set out on a journey without asking pardon of a fellow wayfarer whom he had offended lord just as a woman man or a lad having washed the head and dressed up would be filled with horror loathing and disgust if the carcass of a snake dog or human being was lung around his neck even so lord i am filled with horror loathing and disgust at this vowel body of mind true it is lord that he in whom mindfulness is not present might set out on a journey without asking pardon of a fellow wayfarer whom he had offended lord just as a man might carry around a bowl of fat full of holes and slits oozing dripping even so lord i carry around this body of mine full of holes and slits oozing dripping true it is lot that he in whom mindfulness of the body's actions is not present might set out on a journey without asking fellow of a pardon of a fellow wayfarer in the holy life whom he had offended then that monk got up from his seat arranged his upper rope over 1 shoulder and fell at the feet of the of the exalted 1 exclaiming lord transgression has overtaken me as a fool a blind man an evil man i have accused the verbal sarikuta untruly vainly lyingly lord let the exalted 1 pardon this transgression of mine as a transgression to the end that i may restrain myself in the future and the buddha said verily monk transgression has overtaken you as a fool as a blind man as an evil man you have accused the available sariputta untruly vainly lyingly but since you have seen the transgression as such and recognized it as such we pardon you thoroughly monk there is growth in the discipline of the aryan for him who seeing and recognizing his transgression as such makes amends to the end that he may be restrained in the future then the exalted 1 said to the variable sarikuta pardon this foolish man before his head splits into 7 pieces even where he stands and the remember sarikuta said lord i do pardon that valuable 1 if he speak thus to me and let him to pardon me as the end of the sutta
commentary: so you see this is quite a moving suta this this stupid monk had he was probably filled with jealousy that sarikuta was so famous and so well respected that he simply blamed the available subject for something that he had not done so sariputta here is giving his lines wrong he's saying that just like earth water fire and wind ear bites feeling no hatred even though you cast dirty things like down urine's bottle pass in blood now if you throw this on the earth water fine wind it does not react it does not it is not filled with order or loading or discuss so available because he is an arahant he has no self he's just like the 4 elements and the simile here is in like the down urine spiritual past and blood now it's like all the wrong accusations and all the uh unwholesome things that you say about him he does not feel hatred he does not react and also he says that he's like a duster that sweeps the road or something does not react now even though he sweeps all the dirty things like the boy or girl of the scavenger class with the begging for his food and when he goes into the town now he assumes a humble demeanor a humble face and then goes in so even even then his reputa is like that oh like a bull with a horns cut a bull with horns is very fierce but without the horns it can do nothing so it's very tame so in the same way the ego has been removed from the arahant and he's completely tame he cannot harm anybody and then lastly he says he looks upon his body with horror just like a carcass or a bowl of fat with holes and slits oozing and dripping so after all this description the monk realized that this person is really and arahant and he asked for forgiveness and buddha asks to forgive him but buddha said he will forgive him if the man asks him because the monk actually should pass available sarikuta for forgiveness not the buddha so in the same way sometimes people read that if you accuse a monk wrongly or you have now it to the towards the manga to be very careful because if the mount is an arya like available sarikuta then your offense is very very great like your karmic offense is very great and the buddha said you are digging a hole to bury yourself in the earlier that we read [Music] that's why generally it is not wise for a lay person to be critical of among them especially 1 who keeps the preception and understands the dumb mom we were at suta number
